---
title: "BCL"
title-block-banner: images/headers/new_website_header_01.jpg
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: html

format:
  html: 
    sidebar: false
    css: [theme.css]
---

# **What we do**

The Behavioral Complexity Lab studies how animals sense, communicate, and make decisions in complex ecological and social systems by pairing intensive fieldwork with transparent, reproducible analytical workflows. Working primarily with wild populations, we combine detailed behavioral and acoustic observations with custom-built sensors, bioacoustics, information theory, network analysis, and AI-assisted methods to quantify how information is generated, transmitted, and transformed in real ecological contexts.

A central focus of the lab is understanding how environmental constraints, social interactions, and measurement choices jointly shape observed patterns of behavior, biodiversity, and community organization. In parallel, we critically examine how analytical decisions, bias, and reproducibility affect scientific inference, developing workflows and teaching tools that improve traceability, robustness, and interpretability in ecological research.


::: {.callout-tip icon=false collapse=false}
## News from the lab

* [Jan. 2026] Zach Holmes (PhD student) received word from Titley Scientific that they will support his work deploying recording units at his field site in Hawaii. 
* [Jan. 2026] Michael Castaño (MS student) begins working on novel applications of AI in tropical ecosystems (funded by UW School of Computing)

:::
